rationale: Within a ~9-second window, a parent cmd.exe (PID 1208, EV-001/EV-002) spawned two separate cmd→powershell chains executing local user enumeration commands: 'Get-LocalUser' (A0, EV-001) and 'Get-WmiObject Win32_UserAccount' (EV-002, EV-003). The rapid, sequential, programmatic-style invocation of two distinct user-enumeration techniques via cmd /c wrappers is consistent with post-exploitation discovery activity (T1087.001). However, all processes run at Medium integrity under corp\user-01 with legitimate system binaries, no obfuscation, no encoded commands, and no evidence of lateral movement, persistence, or data exfiltration. The grandparent cmd.exe (PID 1208) with a bare 'cmd.exe' command line is unexplained and warrants investigation to determine what spawned it and whether this is scripted attacker tooling or an authorized admin/IT script.
